imageDescription: Woman in blue dress standing with hands resting near her waist/pockets; no distinctive handshape clearly visible to confirm a specific animal sign
notes: Image shows a person in a neutral pose with no clearly readable gesture (no horn shape, no milking motion). Cannot confirm or disprove the 'Cow' label from the picture alone; keeping acholiLabel from existing glossary (dyang=cow) but flagging for human review since the pose is generic.

imageDescription: Woman in pink dress standing with hands on hips; no clearly distinctive handshape for a specific animal sign
notes: Generic standing pose with hands on hips; no beard/horn gesture visible to confirm 'Goat'. Cannot confidently verify or replace the label from the image alone; flagging for human review. Acholi term (dyel) kept from existing glossary.

imageDescription: A simple analog clock face with hour/minute hands and numerals
notes: Originally labeled 'Animal: Sheep' but the image is clearly a clock face, not a sheep. Relabeled to 'Clock' and moved from animals to time category.

imageDescription: Man making a gesture with curled fingers near his nose/mouth, upward arrow indicating motion
notes: Hand-to-nose gesture is consistent with a 'snout' style sign for Pig; kept the existing label as a plausible Type A sign illustration.

imageDescription: Man with both index fingers pointing upward near his temples/forehead
notes: Two fingers pointing up near the temples does not obviously match common 'Dog' signs (typically a leg-pat or finger-snap). Cannot confidently determine the intended sign from the picture; flagging for human review rather than guessing a replacement label. Acholi term (gwok) kept from existing glossary.

imageDescription: Woman with both hands raised near her cheeks, fingers spread and pointing outward like whiskers, with motion arrows moving away from the face
notes: Classic 'whiskers' handshape at the cheeks matches the common sign for Cat; label confirmed accurate.

imageDescription: Man resting his head sideways on his clasped hands, eyes closed, in a sleeping pose
notes: Originally labeled 'Animal: Chicken' but the image clearly shows a person resting their head on clasped hands with closed eyes - the universal 'Sleep' sign, not a chicken. Relabeled to 'Sleep' and moved to actions category.

imageDescription: Man with one hand held flat near his chest/chin area, thumb extended
notes: Hand position near chin could represent a duck-bill shape but it is not clearly iconic. Cannot confidently verify the label from the image; flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man standing with arms crossed at the wrists in front of his body, motion arrows pointing outward and downward from each hand
notes: Crossed-arm gesture with outward motion arrows does not clearly resemble a flying/wing-flapping motion typically used for 'Bird'. Cannot confidently confirm or replace the label; flagging for human review. Acholi term winyo (bird) is a useful common glossary addition regardless.

imageDescription: A small cropped fragment showing only part of a forearm/elbow against a shirt sleeve; no hand shape or gesture is visible
notes: Image is an illegible cropped fragment (forearm only, no visible hand or sign content) - not a usable vocabulary illustration. Rejected as non-sign reference material.

imageDescription: Man with one arm extended to the side ending in a fist, with an arrow showing horizontal movement across the body
notes: A fist moving horizontally could loosely suggest a slithering motion for 'Snake' but is not a strong match to typical wavy/zigzag snake signs. Flagging for human review rather than asserting a replacement. Acholi term twol (snake) is a useful common glossary addition.

imageDescription: Man with both thumbs-up fists held near his chest, an arrow pointing toward his chest
notes: Thumbs-up gesture near the chest does not have an obvious connection to 'Insect'. Cannot confidently verify or replace the label; flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with arms crossed at the wrists in front of his chest, one thumb visible, with a curved motion arrow
notes: Crossed-arm gesture with a curved arrow does not clearly depict a buzzing/flying motion typically associated with 'Bee'. Flagging for human review. Acholi term kic (bee) is a useful glossary addition.

imageDescription: Man with one thumb-up fist held near his chest and an arrow pointing left/outward
notes: Thumb-up gesture near the chest with an outward arrow does not clearly resemble a fluttering-wings motion typically used for 'Butterfly'. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Woman with both hands raised near her chest, fingers pinched together as if snapping/flicking, with small motion marks
notes: Quick pinching/flicking finger motion near the body is plausibly consistent with the small, darting motion of a 'Fly'; kept existing label as a reasonable Type A illustration.

imageDescription: Cropped view of an arm and hand forming an 'OK'-style handshape (three fingers extended, thumb and index forming a circle) near where a face would be
notes: Image is cropped, showing only a hand with an 'OK'-type handshape near where the face/mane would be. Cannot confidently confirm this matches a 'Lion' sign (often a mane-framing gesture) due to the crop; flagging for human review. Acholi term (labwor) kept from existing glossary.

imageDescription: Man with one hand held flat/open and the other hand's finger touching or pointing at it
notes: Two-handed gesture (finger pointing/touching the other palm) has no obvious iconic link to 'Leopard'. Flagging for human review rather than guessing.

imageDescription: Woman with a fist held near her chest, with a spiral/curved line and arrow tracing downward from the fist
notes: A curved/spotted-pattern line tracing down from the chest could loosely suggest a spotted-coat animal (Cheetah or Leopard), but it is too ambiguous to confirm. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with both hands raised near his forehead/temples in claw shapes, with up and down motion arrows on either side
notes: Bilateral hand gesture near the head with motion arrows is plausibly consistent with a large-ears/trunk style sign for 'Elephant'; kept existing label.

imageDescription: Man holding up one hand in an 'OK'-style handshape (circle formed by thumb and index finger, other fingers extended) near his face
notes: The 'OK'-style handshape near the face does not have an obvious iconic connection to 'Giraffe' (no tall-neck gesture visible). Flagging for human review. Acholi 'twiga' is a common Swahili loanword used in everyday Acholi speech.

imageDescription: Woman with one hand held flat, palm up and fingers extended, and the other hand below it pointing downward with a small arrow
notes: Two-handed gesture (flat hand with pointing hand below) has no clear iconic connection to 'Zebra' (e.g. no stripe-tracing motion). Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Woman with both hands curved outward at waist height, forming a large rounded/oval shape in front of her body
notes: Both hands tracing a large rounded shape in front of the body is plausibly consistent with depicting a large-bodied animal like 'Hippo'; kept existing label.

imageDescription: Woman with both hands on her hips, fingers/thumbs pointing forward, with double horizontal motion arrows
notes: Hands-on-hips pose with horizontal arrows does not clearly depict a horn gesture typically associated with 'Rhino'. Flagging for human review. Acholi 'faru' is a common Swahili-derived loanword.

imageDescription: Man with one fist held at waist/chin level, with a double-headed vertical motion arrow beside it
notes: A single fist with a vertical double arrow does not have an obvious iconic link to 'Buffalo' (e.g. no horn-curve gesture at the head). Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Woman with one finger touching near her mouth/chin, head tilted slightly
notes: Single finger near the chin/mouth does not have a clear iconic link to 'Monkey' (commonly signed with a scratching motion at the torso). Flagging for human review. Acholi term (abuya) kept from existing glossary.

imageDescription: Man tracing an arc with one finger from his chest down to his other wrist, with a motion arrow
notes: An arcing finger-trace from chest to wrist does not match the common chest-beating sign for 'Gorilla'. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Woman with both hands forming thumbs-up fists held near her chest, with vertical motion arrows on both sides suggesting a repeated up-down motion
notes: Both fists tapping/beating rhythmically near the chest closely matches the common chest-beating sign used for great apes (Chimpanzee/Gorilla); kept existing label as a plausible Type A illustration.

imageDescription: A small cropped fragment showing only a shirt pocket and part of a sleeve; no hand or gesture visible

imageDescription: A small cropped fragment showing only a forearm/elbow against a shirt sleeve with a fingertip and small motion arrow visible; no full gesture or face visible
notes: Image is a small illegible cropped fragment (forearm only) with no recognizable full gesture - not a usable vocabulary illustration. Rejected as non-sign reference material.

imageDescription: Man with one finger touching his temple/forehead in a 'thinking' gesture
notes: Originally labeled 'Animal: Lizard' but the image clearly shows a person touching their temple in the universal 'Think' gesture, unrelated to a lizard. Relabeled to 'Think' and moved to actions category.

imageDescription: Woman with two fingers raised near her mouth/chin, mouth open as if speaking or croaking
notes: Fingers near the open mouth could loosely suggest a croaking gesture for 'Frog' but this is not a strongly iconic match. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Woman with both hands raised near her chest, fingers spread and slightly curled with small motion marks, suggesting wiggling movement
notes: Both hands with spread, wiggling fingers closely matches the common 'crawling legs' sign for 'Spider'; kept existing label.

imageDescription: Man with one hand near his mouth, fingers pinched together as if pinching/biting

imageDescription: A small cropped fragment showing only a shirt pocket and part of a collar; no hand or gesture visible

imageDescription: Man with both arms extended out to the sides, hands open, with motion arrows pointing diagonally downward/outward
notes: Both arms spread outward with downward arrows does not have an obvious iconic link to a small creature like 'Ant'. Flagging for human review. Acholi term biye (ant) is a useful glossary addition regardless.

imageDescription: Man with one hand extended, fingers pointing downward with a small motion arrow beneath them
notes: Downward-pointing fingers with a small arrow does not clearly depict a buzzing/flying motion for 'Bee'. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with one hand raised near his face, fingers curled in an 'O' shape, with an upward motion arrow beside it

imageDescription: Man with both hands extended downward in front of him, fingers curled, with downward motion arrows on both sides

imageDescription: Woman with one hand near her cheek/jaw and a dashed-line tracing the outline of her arm and hand, with an arrow pointing toward her cheek
notes: A hand-to-cheek gesture with a traced motion path does not have an obvious iconic link to 'Locust'. Flagging for human review. Acholi term ayer (locust) is a useful glossary addition regardless, as locusts are a familiar seasonal food/pest in Acholi culture.

imageDescription: Woman with one hand near her shoulder/chest and a curved blue arc/wave motion line beside her
notes: A hand near the shoulder with a curved motion line does not have a clear iconic connection to 'Cockroach'. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with both hands raised near his chest, fingers pinched together, with upward motion arrows on both sides
notes: Both hands with pinched fingers rising upward is plausibly consistent with depicting swarming termites/flying white ants emerging; kept existing label. Acholi term ngwen (termite/white ant) is a common and culturally familiar word.

imageDescription: Man with arms crossed at the wrists in front of his body, with a curved motion arrow

notes: Image is an illegible cropped fragment (shoulder/sleeve only, no visible hand or sign content) - not a usable vocabulary illustration. Rejected as non-sign reference material.

imageDescription: Man with both hands raised near his face, palms forward, with curved lines arcing over his head from one hand to the other
notes: Both raised hands with arcing lines over the head does not have an obvious iconic connection to 'Fin'. Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with one hand stroking/rubbing along the wrist and forearm of the other arm, with a small arrow
notes: A stroking/rubbing motion along the arm is plausibly consistent with showing a textured surface for 'Scale'; kept existing label as a reasonable Type A illustration.

imageDescription: Man with both hands cupped together, one hand forming a rounded shape with the other hand resting against it, and an upward motion arrow
notes: A cupped, rounded handshape is plausibly consistent with depicting a 'Shell'; kept existing label as a reasonable Type A illustration.

imageDescription: Man holding one hand up vertically beside his face, palm facing outward, fingers together
notes: A flat vertical hand held beside the face does not have an obvious iconic connection to an oval 'Egg' shape. Flagging for human review. Acholi term (tong) kept from existing glossary.

imageDescription: A small cropped fragment showing only a forearm/elbow against a shirt sleeve; no hand shape or gesture visible
notes: Image is an illegible cropped fragment (forearm only, no visible hand or sign content) - not a usable vocabulary illustration. Rejected as non-sign reference material.

imageDescription: Man pointing downward with one hand, an arrow above pointing down toward his hand, looking forward
notes: A pointing-downward gesture does not have an obvious iconic connection to 'Cage' (e.g. no bars/enclosure shape). Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with one hand sweeping in an arc near his chin/jaw, with a curved motion arrow
notes: An arcing hand sweep near the chin does not have an obvious iconic connection to 'Stable' (animal shelter). Flagging for human review.

imageDescription: Man with both hands held at waist level in fists, with a curved line connecting them below, suggesting an enclosed/boundary area
notes: Both hands tracing a broad curved area at waist level is plausibly consistent with indicating an expansive area like 'Forest/Wild'; kept existing label. Acholi term tim (forest/bush/wild) is a common, well-established word.

imageDescription: Simple line drawing of a hand with index finger extended pointing upward, with an additional upward arrow beside it
notes: Simple upward-pointing handshape illustration is a plausible abstract sign for 'Week'; label kept. Acholi term cabit (week) is a useful glossary addition - already referenced indirectly in the UI badge names.

imageDescription: Simple line drawing of a hand with two fingers extended pointing upward, with arrows beside them
notes: Simple two-finger upward handshape illustration is a plausible abstract sign for 'Month'; label kept. Acholi term dwe (month/moon) is a useful glossary addition.

imageDescription: Line drawing of two cupped hands with an upward motion arrow, suggesting a cyclical or repeating gesture
notes: Two-handed cyclical gesture is a plausible abstract sign for 'Year'; label kept. Acholi term mwaka (year) is a common, widely used word.

imageDescription: Line drawing of two hands with fingers extended downward, with double vertical arrows below each hand
notes: Repeated two-handed downward gesture is a plausible abstract sign for a longer time span like 'Decade'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'mwaka apar' (ten years), combining glossary terms mwaka (year) and apar (ten).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a person's arm with a hand making a repeated downward sweeping/scooping motion, with double motion arrows
notes: Repeated sweeping arm gesture is a plausible abstract sign for an even longer time span like 'Century'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'mwaka miya acel' (one hundred years).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a person with both arms bent at the elbow, fists near the body, in a static pose
notes: Static two-armed pose with hands held back near the body is a plausible abstract gesture for 'Past' (looking/gesturing backward); label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'kare ma okatre' (time that has passed).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a hand with fingers spread, viewed from the wrist down
notes: Simple open-hand illustration is a plausible abstract sign for 'Present' (here/now); label kept. Acholi term kombeddi (now/present) is a common, well-known word.

imageDescription: Line drawing of a hand with fingers slightly curled, viewed from the wrist down, similar style to the 'Present' illustration
notes: Simple hand illustration in the same style as nearby time-concept signs is a plausible abstract sign for 'Future'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'kare mabikato' (time that will come).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a hand held flat with fingers together, angled downward, partially overlapping another hand shape
notes: Simple flat-hand illustration is a plausible abstract sign for 'Early'; label kept. Acholi term con (early/before) is a commonly used word.

imageDescription: Line drawing of a hand positioned near a shoulder/arm, with a downward-curving motion arrow
notes: Hand-to-shoulder gesture with downward motion is a plausible abstract sign for 'Late'; label kept.

imageDescription: Line drawing of a hand with fingers extended near a face/chin, with curved motion arrows above
notes: Hand-to-face gesture with arrows is a plausible abstract sign for 'On time'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'i kare mere' (at its proper time).

imageDescription: Line drawing of two hands clasped together, one hand pressing down on the other with a curved motion arrow
notes: Two-handed clasped gesture with motion is a plausible abstract sign for 'Duration'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'anywang kare' (length of time).

imageDescription: Line drawing of two hands pressed together with fingers interlocking/overlapping
notes: Two interlocked hands forming a continuous/unbroken shape is a plausible abstract sign for 'Forever'; label kept. Acholi term matwal (forever/always) is a common, well-known word.

imageDescription: Line drawing of one hand resting on top of the other, with a small arrow pointing toward the fingers
notes: One hand resting/tapping on the other is a plausible abstract sign for 'Sometimes'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'i kare mukene' (at other times).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a hand/foot shape with fingers/toes spread, partially overlapping another similar shape
notes: Simple spread-fingers illustration is a plausible abstract sign for 'Never'; label kept. Acholi rendered as 'tutwal pe' (not at all), building on the glossary term 'pe' (no).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a person with both hands held together near the cheek, eyes closed, head tilted - a resting/sleeping-like pose
notes: Repetitive resting-pose illustration is a plausible abstract sign for 'Always' (a routine/repeated action); label kept. Acholi term 'pile pile' (always/every day) is a common, well-known phrase.

imageDescription: Line drawing of an open mouth with teeth visible and a finger moving back and forth in front of it, with horizontal double arrows
notes: Repeated back-and-forth finger motion in front of the mouth is a plausible abstract sign for 'Frequently'; label kept. Acholi rendered as 'pile pile' (often/repeatedly), same as 'Always' since Acholi often uses this phrase for both repeated concepts.

imageDescription: Line drawing of two hands, one cupped beneath the other with curled fingers, and a small curved motion arrow
notes: Small, contained two-handed gesture is a plausible abstract sign for 'Rarely' (a small/limited amount); label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'i kare manok' (at few times), building on the glossary term 'manok' (few/small).

imageDescription: Line drawing of a person with both arms raised, hands open near the shoulders, an arrow pointing up from the upper left
notes: Two-handed raised gesture is a plausible abstract sign for 'Calendar'; label kept. Acholi 'kalenda' is a widely used loanword for calendar.

imageDescription: Line drawing of two hands clasped together with one hand on top of the other, fingers interlocking, with a curved arrow
notes: Two-handed clasped gesture is a plausible abstract sign for 'Schedule'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'ratela me tic' (plan/order of work), as there is no traditional one-word equivalent for the modern concept of a schedule.

imageDescription: Line drawing of a person with both arms raised toward the shoulders/face, hands open, similar two-armed gesture to the 'Calendar' illustration
notes: Two-handed raised gesture in the same illustration style as 'Calendar' and 'Schedule' is a plausible abstract sign for 'Appointment'; label kept. Acholi rendered descriptively as 'kare ma kiketo' (time that is set/arranged), as there is no traditional one-word equivalent for this modern concept.
